question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/GrpcApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: retryWhen() in RedisTopicListener] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the Retry.backoff(Long.MAX_VALUE, interval).maxBackoff(interval.multipliedBy(4L)) in RedisTopicListener by subscribing during a Redis network partition, causing the retry loop to hold a reactive scheduler thread indefinitely and prevent recovery of the Redis connection?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/GrpcApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: health() in GrpcHealthIndicator] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that GrpcHealthIndicator.health() reflects only gRPC server lifecycle events (not Redis or DB connectivity), causing health checks to report UP while the service is actually partitioned from its data sources, masking a network partition from operators?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/GrpcApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic() in TopicMessageServiceImpl] Can an unprivileged external user open a subscribeTopic() stream with no endTime and no limit, then hold the TCP connection open without reading, causing the gRPC server to buffer responses indefinitely and exhaust heap memory, which partitions the JVM from the network?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/GrpcApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: poll() in SharedPollingTopicListener] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that SharedPollingTopicListener uses a single PollingContext.lastConsensusTimestamp shared across all subscribers, such that a subscriber that causes the poll to fail resets the timestamp state and causes all other subscribers to miss messages, simulating a partition?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/GrpcApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: retryWhen() in PollingTopicMessageRetriever] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the Retry.backoff(Long.MAX_VALUE, Duration.ofSeconds(1)) in PollingTopicMessageRetriever by subscribing to a topic during a DB network partition, causing the retry loop to accumulate and hold Schedulers.boundedElastic() threads, preventing the service from recovering the DB connection?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/GrpcApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic() in TopicMessageServiceImpl] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the subscriberCount AtomicLong by opening and closing subscriptions rapidly, causing the counter to increment/decrement at high frequency and generating metric recording overhead that starves the Schedulers.boundedElastic() pool and contributes to a partition?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/GrpcApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: toFilter() in ConsensusController] Can an unprivileged external user send a ConsensusTopicQuery with consensusStartTime.nanos set to Integer.MAX_VALUE and consensusStartTime.seconds set to a large value, causing DomainUtils.timestampInNanosMax() to produce a startTime that overflows and causes the polling loop to behave unexpectedly, contributing to a partition?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/GrpcApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribe() in RedisTopicListener] Can an unprivileged external user subscribe to a topic that has never received any messages, causing the RedisTopicListener to hold an open Redis subscription channel indefinitely with no data flowing, and when done at scale for many distinct topic IDs, exhaust Redis connection slots and partition Redis from the service?
